ps140: Come, O divinely wise, and let us be transformed* with a goodly transformation today,* piously and joyously being transfigured with Christ.* And, borne up to the summit of the virtues,* let us rejoice with yet greater exaltation;* for the Saviour of our souls, in being transfigured,* has in His lovingkindness enlightened the ugliness of man.

O you who love the sight of things which transcend comprehension,* O you who love obedience!* Let us noetically look upon Christ, who has shone forth with divine radiance,* and let us cry out with the voice of His Father,* proclaiming Him the beloved Son who strengthened human nature on Tabor* and has poured forth enlightenment for our souls.

Today, O Lord, You have shone forth Your divinity* to Your apostles, and to Moses and Elijah,* in that You are the God of the law and of grace,* delivering us now from the bonds of death.* And with them we glorify Your loving dispensation,* O almighty Jesus, Saviour of our souls.

You openly venerated the image of Christ, O all-wise one,* when the cruelty of Leo burst into flame and troubled the people,* reviving the ungodly heresy of Copronymus.* Then you openly denounced him,* taking your stand boldly, when the hierarchs came together.* Wherefore, you were shown to be a most valiant warrior of Christ, O most blessed one.* Entreat Him that He save and enlighten our souls.

O you who have made your abode in the mansions of heaven:* when the ungodly and impious one showed forth his adverse character,* he condemned your steadfastness to imprisonment.* Then, O divinely eloquent one,* who manifestly knew the one homeland of paradise,* you did in no wise fall away therefrom,* but, rejoicing with gladness, endured.* Wherefore, you have been rightly counted worthy to behold Christ, the Life incorruptible.* Entreat Him that He save and enlighten our souls.

When the accursed Leo imposed his vile alien law,* commanding that no one venerate the precious image of Christ,* then you, most wisely possessed of a tongue of fire,* set against it the law of God,* for the veneration accorded to the icons passes over to the Prototype, as someone once said.* And the foul beast was affrighted by your discourse, as though by thunder,* O most honourable, divinely eloquent, and sacred father.

He who of old spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai in images, saying: “I am He who is”,* today, transfigured before His disciples on Mount Tabor,* has shown forth the pristine beauty of His countenance,* having taken upon Himself human nature.* And having set before them Moses and Elijah as witnesses to this grace,* He made them partakers of gladness who, for the sake of the Cross,* proclaim His glorious and saving resurrection.
aposticha: Tabor, the mountain of God,* rejoices in Your name, O Saviour,* as the Father proclaims You* to be His beloved Son.
A wonder was it to behold the material sun hide its rays* at the transfiguration of the Sun of glory.
When You were transfigured,* the disciples, from among those on earth,* Moses, from among the dead,* and Elijah, as one from above,* stood before You as servants.

David, the ancestor of God,* foreseeing in the Spirit the coming of Your Only-begotten Son in the flesh to men,* summons creation from afar to gladness, and cries out prophetically:* “Tabor and Hermon shall rejoice in Your name!”* For, having ascended that mountain with Your disciples, O Saviour,* You were transfigured, and caused the darkened nature of Adam to shine again,* imparting to it the glory and splendour of Your divinity.* Wherefore, we cry to You: O Lord, Creator of all things, glory be to You!
